assertions.title: PostHog is dynamically imported
assertions.reason: The inspected client entrypoint and analytics hook both load posthog-js with dynamic import() instead of a static top-level client import.

evidence.details: PosthogInit calls void import('posthog-js').then(({ default: posthog }) => { ... }) inside useEffect, so the browser loads posthog-js after hydration and only when PostHog config exists.
The hook memoizes a Promise<typeof import('posthog-js')> and assigns posthogPromise = import('posthog-js') on first use, preserving on-demand loading for capture paths.
A fixed-string search for static from 'posthog-js' imports returned no matches, so the inspected TypeScript client files do not statically import posthog-js.

assertions.issues: Non-blocking gap: the fire-and-forget dynamic import chains shown in entry.client.tsx and use-analytics.ts do not attach .catch(), so a failed chunk request could leave analytics unavailable and surface an unhandled promise rejection.

assertions.title: recharts is lazily loaded on admin stats
assertions.reason: The admin stats route lazy-loads each recharts-based chart module with React.lazy() and renders the chart grid behind a Suspense fallback, keeping recharts imports inside the split modules.

evidence.details: The route defines MonthlyActiveUsersChart, AdminStatsSignerConversionChart, and AdminStatsUsersWithDocumentsChart with lazy(async () => import(...).then((mod) => ({ default: mod.ComponentName }))).
The chart section is wrapped in <Suspense> with animated placeholder cards, so the route can render while the chart chunks load asynchronously.
This lazy-loaded chart module imports TooltipProps plus Bar, BarChart, ResponsiveContainer, Tooltip, XAxis, and YAxis from recharts.
This lazy-loaded chart module imports its chart primitives directly from recharts.
This lazy-loaded chart module imports Bar/BarChart/ResponsiveContainer/Tooltip/XAxis/YAxis and tooltip types from recharts.

summary: Both assigned bundle-size assertions pass on read-only terminal inspection. posthog-js is dynamically imported in the client entry and analytics hook, and the admin stats route lazy-loads the recharts chart modules behind a Suspense boundary.
